description: Lock Public Anatomy Theaters to university dissection-teaching venues, not printed scientific illustration media.

checks.reason: Vesalius-era detailed anatomy is the dissection-centered teaching content for public anatomy theaters.
Vesalian anatomy is teaching context, not the built venue itself.
The Padua exemplar is a university anatomy teaching structure.
Medical faculties provide institutional teaching context, while the venue design is the direct built requirement.
Anatomical theater design is the same-year built-form context for the public dissection venue.
The built anatomy-theater design is a same-year sibling venue concept, not an earlier hard prerequisite.
Scientific illustration printing should not be restored unless the node is narrowed to printed anatomical teaching media.
The node remains anchored to the University of Padua anatomical theatre construction date.
